    • EDA: Graph-based Features from Spatial Cell Data

Import cell-graph topology from a PyTables HDF5 file

Source: R/importGraphH5.R
importGraphH5.Rd

Reads the nodes and edges datasets from a PyTables-formatted .h5 file produced by the cell-graph construction pipeline and optionally converts the result to an igraph object.

Usage

importGraphH5(fname, as_igraph = TRUE, patient_id = NULL)

Arguments

fname

Character(1). Path to a .h5 graph file. The file must contain two PyTables datasets: nodes (column node_id) and edges (columns source and target).

as_igraph

Logical(1). If TRUE (default), return an igraph object. If FALSE, return a list with nodes and edges data frames.

patient_id

Character(1) or NULL. Patient identifier stored as a graph-level attribute. Inferred from the TCGA barcode in fname when NULL.

Value

An undirected igraph object (or list when as_igraph = FALSE) with the following graph-level attributes:

patient_id

TCGA patient barcode.

source_file

Absolute path of the source file.

n_nodes

Number of cell nodes.

n_edges

Number of edges.

Details

The .h5 files use the PyTables HDF5 format written by pandas.DataFrame.to_hdf(). This function reads them via reticulate using h5py to access the raw HDF5 datasets and pandas to construct data frames — the tables (PyTables) back-end is not required. Ensure that a Python environment with h5py >= 3.0 and pandas >= 1.3 is available before calling this function (e.g. pip install h5py pandas).

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
h5_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_graph.h5", package = "CellGraphR")
g <- importGraphH5(h5_file)
igraph::vcount(g)   # number of cell nodes
igraph::ecount(g)   # number of cell-cell edges
igraph::graph_attr(g, "patient_id")
} # }
